other_observers: none; found and identified by me
description: the bird was about the same size as Yellow-rumped Warblers it was associating with. It was also basically the same shape, but with a relatively shorter tail and slightly less chunky look giving it a slightly slimmer appearance. The bird had fairly long primary projection. The bill was short, thin and pointed, and black. The legs were dark gray or black.  The crown and nape of the bird were dusky olive-greenish. The bird had a lightly contrasting yellower eyebrow, and a dusky eye-line. The throat, sides of the neck, and chest were buffy-yellowish in color, yellowest on the neck and buffier towards the lower chest and sides. The flanks were rufous, widest towards the lower part and tapering towards the bend of the wing. The belly was whitish with a slighly buffy-yellow wash. The undertail coverts were similar in color to the chest. The underside of the tail appeared to have large white patches, but I didn't get a good look at this mark.  The back of the bird was dusky olive in color, with black streaks. The wings were dark gray with two bold white wing bars. The tail was also dark gray.Behaviors: The bird was associating with a large flock of Yellow-rumped Warblers, though it spent all of its time at mid levels in the trees and never came to the ground or water drip as the Yellow-rumped often did.Call: none heardPlumage: basic
